core_model.structure_summary: Minimization of an integrated cost over all joint couplings of two fixed marginal measures, where the cost is a parametric monotone transform of a pairwise value function and the parameter tunes concavity.
core_model.canonical_form: \min_{\pi \in \Pi(\mu,\nu)} \int_{X\times Y} c_\alpha(x,y)\, d\pi(x,y), \qquad c_\alpha(x,y) = \tfrac{1}{\alpha}\bigl(1 - e^{\alpha\, U(x,y)}\bigr)

variables.meaning: a joint coupling (transport plan) between two spaces with prescribed marginals
an inequality-aversion index controlling the concavity of the cost transform, and the underlying pairwise value function
expected transformed pairwise value integrated against the coupling

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A Kantorovich optimal-transport problem: minimize an integrated pairwise cost over couplings with fixed marginals, whose optimum is characterized by cyclical monotonicity.

notes: The stability/matching notions are recast as a parametric family of optimal transport (Kantorovich) linear programs over couplings; the parametric Atkinson-cost family is the core, the bare optimal-transport problem is its skeleton. The graph/perfect-matching probability bounds near the end are auxiliary approximation results.
